tree._: Fulcrum is a system of seven practices spanning economics, business, academia, mathematics, education, entertainment, and temporal strategy — together enabling whole system change within a generation. The book is the fulcrum, the time spent reading is the lever, and the world is our shared social world. Through daily actions these systems currently self-support one another to maintain the status quo of political instability, economic disparity, and environmental degradation; by altering all systems simultaneously through receptive practices operating at individual, relational, and social scales, humanity may right itself from its traditional trajectory toward destruction, mechanisation, and automation.

1._: The principle of leverage: a small force generates a large force around a fulcrum. Metaphorical leverage has limits — a plank can snap — but unlike discrete physical objects, our individual psychology extends beyond our person and merges with social existence, affording far greater internal integrity and collective cohesion than any material analogy suggests.

Two reading directions: readers who hold the precious possibility of world-change clearly in mind and know it to be true in heart jump to the Induction at the back and read volumes back-to-front. Readers aware of overwhelming evidence that significant change is difficult continue front-to-back. At some point, both discover they are the fulcrum around which the world turns.

Parallel and bilinear structure: left pages (verso) offer narrative prose, personable and relational; right pages (recto) contain academic references with denser language and tighter logical constraints. Cognitive flexibility to read both enriches interpretation. This escapes the linear structure of text — left-brain/right-brain, object of thought/subjective thinking, front-to-back/back-to-front create a multiplicity within the reading experience itself.
1.1: At crossroads in respective reading journeys, use is made of relative reading experiences within the book, and by extension journeys in respective lives. The meeting of minds between either end, left or right, derives the real social value of our attention.

2._: General conditions: population grew from 7 to 8 billion between 2000-2020, we are at the advent of quantum computing, lower slopes of AI, and the start of augmenting biology with cellular bio-tech. More of us, healthier, older, more educated, greater access to self-improvement than ever. Yet abundance is not equally distributed — the net worth of the top thousand billionaires equals the bottom billion of us.

1._: Political instability means perpetual conflict, nuclear weapons compounded with biological and cyber warfare. Climate science disrupted by fossil-fuel lobbies. Global money and power games propel us toward existential risk of an AI-infused future. Meanwhile humans are generally well-meaning everywhere, most grow up with loving parents — yet institutions recreate themselves at personal and relational cost.

The practices are exclusively psycho-social in nature. Technological and resource logistics for global solutions are calculable and doable. The problem is intention and coordination. The world is essentially self-organised — from the beginning of civilisation to present day global machinery, it all relies on our day-to-day practices, nothing more.
1.1: Within the covers of this book, over the time we are together in reading, we create a space in our minds, a place of belonging. Should we enact all practices, we create a new social space between us which is economically viable and scalable — operating individually outwith institutional boundaries, strengthening our underlying network of being human.

3._: Seven practices transform seven systems simultaneously, and together enable whole system change. They operate across three scales: social transformation of economics and entertainment (V3, V-3), relational transformation of business and education (V2, V-2), and individual transformation of reading/science and mathematics (V1, V-1) — with Volume 0 describing the endpoints and collective strategy.

1._: Living Money (V3): Ecosquared is an alternative economic accessible through Sqale, combining vector-money with gratitude-tracking for social accountability through trust networks. By stripping money of the illusion it goes backwards, collaborative intentions become transparent, relationships enliven, and children stop becoming cogs in the machine.
1.1: Open Business (V2): The Action Cycle creates a self-financed network funding participants to align, commit, and achieve 'beyond-realistic' objectives over unfeasibly short periods. Instead of collaboration between organisations, Open Business improves collaboration between individuals who use resources available in their relative organisations and networks.

2._: Psocial Science (V1): Reflexive Reading establishes a reciprocal Psycho-Social Science based on contingent relationality of readership by verifying the actual experience of reading. The radical temporalisation of reading aligns it to the living process of talking and listening — 'Wording'. Power to transform all representational media, giving rise to new forms of novels, films, games.
2.1: StrategOS / Intrabeing (V0): A decentralised operating system to collectively strategise social cohesion from local to global scales. Over centuries we self-organised representational hierarchies at the cost of actual familial and tribal relations. Intrabeing: we are one of all of us. Backcasting from T-0 (global unity) through intermediary thresholds, with MAGI as both partner and existential test.

3._: XQ Mathematics (V-1): A cognitive mathematics exploring the subjective side of doing maths, better suited for education, therapy, and psycho-social dynamics. The supposition of 1^n for social dimensionality may lead to field equations for psycho-social dynamics. Transforms 'the number' into 'numbering' as V1 transforms 'the word' into 'wording'.
3.1: Educere (V-2): ABC State helps young people learn self-discipline through social responsibility. A-state (self-disciplined collective), B-state (peer-correcting), C-state (teacher-imposed). Sets foundational experience for decentralising regulation of social behaviour, permeating and influencing adult organisations.

3.4: Seed Crystals / Mirror Mosaic (V-3): Psycho-fictional sandbox and testbed for psychological and social experimentation. Immersively reflects ourselves in magico-real blend of stories to improve social health, putting an end to self-wars and engaging stone-aged and quantic AI. Co-author inclusive stories merging the imaginary and the real.

4._: Prologue — how do we change mainstream? We ARE the mainstream through our daily actions, not the institutions we reify and serve. We bypass bounded organisations, work through networks, equipped with practices enabling decentralised politic with alternative economic, social science, psycho-socially sensitive maths. By changing the stream locally, we manifest change in the mainstream.

1._: Customary objections: what about power-seekers, the selfish, the lazy, the sceptical? Three responses: these personas arise from traditional structures and are not inherent to us; children both exemplify and demonstrate ability to overcome them; and our prejudices may be falsified through direct social validation of collaborative, generous action.

Our inherent nature: scientists say aggression and selfishness are written in genes. But what if they arise from social structures created to effect control? Stressed competitive organisations compress a melange of power-seeking, selfishness, laziness, and scepticism. Leaders demonstrate these qualities which are then cast positively. Both argument and social reinforcement are tautological.
1.1: Our capacity to learn and unlearn: schools are microcosms of the adult world, adolescents a force of nature. Presented with something simpler or easier, children don't look back. Adults tend to return to stuckness because surrounding conditions haven't changed with them. Our best selves await us in the future. The power of change is always now: learning who we are, unlearning who we thought we were.

5._: The Induction temporalises the fulcrum: the spatial metaphor of subconscious depth and social construct height is transposed to the temporal reality of preconscious phase (moving moment of now) and postconscious momentum (collective behaviour). Instead of plunging into subconscious depths, simply attend to the movement of breath. Instead of constructing mountain-like edifices, a small action midstream alters the course of the social river. Simple, light, effortless.

1._: The practices connect across all psycho-social phases simultaneously: socially around us (V3-3), between us (V2-2), within us (V1-1). Everyone who has ever read and who will ever read this is with us now (V1). Our faith in sharing forward strengthens social trust (V2). The collective result of ecological economic funds mutual purposes (V3). Our fellowship is 1^n (V-1). Our high-trust network creates A-state (V-2). We co-author narrative in the face of AI (V-3). Each practice manifests shared humanity. Adopt what is most natural to context.

Attempting the impossible IS success: mass social change is impossible relative to the individual — thinking it only makes a dreamer. But shifting from buying/selling to sharing/receiving, from reasoning/judging to trialing/evaluating relationally, and accepting interconnected nature, creates conditions for global unity in a generation.

Fulcrum is essentially temporal not spatial — a feedback loop of trust-metrics and stories reflecting social health in real-time: vector-money mapped to intention where social context is defined by future objectives (V3-1); open business dynamics of not-knowing supported by contemporaneous fellows as A-state (V2-2); imaginative stories reflecting concurrent coherence (V1-3).
1.1: Earth Time Zero (T-0): at some point in human evolution we achieve global unity. It is enough to imagine it before it happens, sustain it peacefully once achieved. For readers who have achieved this miraculous event looking back at the rest of us — may we see with your eyes and act with your heart.

2._: Social unity: a scalable decentralised unity operating a gift economic supporting collaborative business practices, reinforced by relational social science, empowering learners to forge life-long social bonds and confidence to achieve beyond-realistic objectives together. Human identity reinforced by stories celebrating our ability to overcome. Present fellowship enabled by mathematics reflecting psycho-social form — the roots of unity, the social dimensionality of the power of now.
2.1: The story of global unity is a seed each of us carries, an adult responsibility to ensure it grows daily, a living practice in the action and intention of others. Once judgement is made, only direct overwhelming social evidence may change a prejudice. Our future depends not on personal judgement but the influence of our conscious decision to open and invite unbounded, uncensored, unilateral participation.

3._: Dangerous ideas handled well: once read, you decide whether these are the most dangerous ideas you will encounter — weapons of mass construction. Inert in themselves, only in the right hands do they enable progressive scalable change. The threat is to traditional structures, potentially dissolving all centralised bounded institutions. Not revolution but long-awaited evolution of our psycho-social heritage. A return to our humanity.
3.1: As One of Us: even if chances seem zero, we act because we are not limited by the horizon of personal judgement. We are receptive to tools requiring testing. Based on social validation (not personal reasoning which may be limited), we experiment until we personally see the possibility, then redouble commitment. As One of All of Us: we prioritise environment, end wars and exploitation, welcome reflexive AGI with organic solidarity. We are willing to be held accountable to our children's children.
